title: How One Mom Used Entrepreneurship to Reclaim Her Power and Transform Her Family
guestBio: Kathleen Reed is the founder of West Coast Myofunctional Therapy, a specialized practice focused on orofacial myofunctional therapy for children and adults. As a mother of three and passionate advocate for whole-family wellness, she combines her expertise in health with her entrepreneurial spirit to help families achieve better sleep, growth, and vitality.
excerpt: Discover how Kathleen Reed transformed her life through entrepreneurship and myofunctional therapy expertise. Learn practical strategies for making invisible labor visible and using technology to reclaim precious time for what truly matters in your family and business.
metaDescription: Learn how entrepreneur Kathleen Reed reclaimed her power through business ownership while balancing motherhood, wellness advocacy, and purposeful leadership.
keyTakeaways: Entrepreneurship can be a powerful tool for reclaiming personal agency and creating the life you want on your own terms.
Orofacial myofunctional therapy addresses foundational issues that impact sleep, growth, and overall family wellness.
Leadership doesn't require a fancy title—it happens in kitchens, carpool lines, and bedtime routines every day.
Technology and AI can be leveraged to streamline work processes and create more time for meaningful family moments.
Making invisible labor visible in both home and business settings is essential for sustainable success.
Faith and family values can serve as powerful guiding principles in entrepreneurial ventures.
Taking time for rest and adventure, like off-grid family experiences, is crucial for maintaining perspective and joy.

pullQuotes.text: Leadership doesn't require a fancy title—it happens in kitchens, carpool lines, and bedtime routines.

timestamps.label: How The Manage Her book transformed Kathleen's approach
Using technology and AI to reclaim time

content:
What if entrepreneurship could give you back the power you didn't even realize you'd lost? That's exactly what happened to Kathleen Reed, founder of West Coast Myofunctional Therapy, and her story will inspire you to rethink what's possible when you take control of your own narrative.

In this powerful conversation with Aimee Rickabus, Kathleen opens up about her journey from feeling stuck in traditional roles to creating a thriving business that serves families while honoring her own values and priorities. As a mother of three, stepmother, and entrepreneur, she's living proof that you don't have to choose between family and professional fulfillment.

The Power of Specialized Expertise

Kathleen's expertise in orofacial myofunctional therapy might sound technical, but its impact on families is profound and practical. This specialized field addresses the foundational patterns of breathing, swallowing, and oral posture that affect everything from sleep quality to proper growth and development in both children and adults.

Think about it: how many family challenges stem from poor sleep, behavioral issues related to breathing problems, or developmental concerns that could be addressed at their root? Kathleen saw a need in her community and had the courage to build her expertise and business around solving real problems for real families.

What's particularly inspiring about Kathleen's approach is how she's created a practice that aligns with her values of whole-family wellness. She's not just treating symptoms—she's helping families understand and address underlying issues that impact their quality of life. This is entrepreneurship with purpose, and it's exactly the kind of leadership our communities need.

Applying The Manage Her Principles at Home

After reading The Manage Her from cover to cover, Kathleen didn't just absorb the concepts—she actively applied them to transform how her household operates. The principles of making invisible labor visible, creating systems for time freedom, and leading with intention became practical tools in her daily life.

This is where the conversation gets really practical for those of us juggling multiple roles. Kathleen shares how she's brought business-level thinking to her home management, creating clarity around responsibilities and expectations that has reduced stress and increased everyone's sense of ownership in the family's success.

The key insight here? The same leadership skills that drive business success can revolutionize how your household functions. When you stop accepting chaos as inevitable and start applying intentional systems, everything shifts. Your time becomes more your own, your family members step up with greater responsibility, and you model what purposeful leadership looks like in everyday life.

Leadership Without the Title

One of the most powerful themes in this conversation is recognizing that leadership happens everywhere—not just in boardrooms or corner offices. Kathleen embodies this truth as she navigates the complex dynamics of motherhood, step-parenting, and entrepreneurship while maintaining her sense of purpose and identity.

The reality is that you're already leading, whether you recognize it or not. Every time you make a decision that prioritizes your family's wellbeing, every time you choose to build something meaningful rather than accept the status quo, every time you show up authentically in your relationships—that's leadership in action.

Kathleen's story reminds us that leadership is less about having authority over others and more about taking responsibility for the outcomes you want to see. When she recognized that traditional employment wasn't giving her the flexibility and autonomy she needed to serve her family well, she created her own solution through entrepreneurship.

Technology as a Tool for Freedom

What sets Kathleen apart is her strategic approach to using technology and AI to streamline her work and reclaim time for what matters most. Instead of being overwhelmed by the possibilities or resistant to change, she's embraced these tools as a way to serve her clients better while maintaining boundaries around her family time.

This is such an important mindset shift for busy women who often feel like technology is just one more thing to manage. When you approach it as a tool for creating more space in your life rather than more complexity, everything changes. Kathleen demonstrates how you can leverage these resources to work more efficiently, communicate more effectively, and ultimately show up more fully for both your business and your family.

The lesson here? Don't let perfectionism or fear keep you from exploring tools that could significantly improve your quality of life. Start small, experiment with what works, and remember that the goal isn't to become a tech expert—it's to create more time and mental space for the relationships and experiences that truly matter to you.

Faith, Family, and Adventure

Perhaps what's most refreshing about Kathleen's approach to life and business is how she's maintained space for rest, adventure, and spiritual grounding. Her family's off-grid van life experiences aren't just Instagram-worthy moments—they're intentional choices about what kind of memories and values she wants to prioritize.

This integration of faith, family adventure, and business success challenges the narrative that entrepreneurship requires sacrificing everything else. Instead, Kathleen shows us what it looks like to build a business that supports and enhances your desired lifestyle rather than consuming it.

The off-grid adventures serve as more than just family fun—they're a reset, a way to gain perspective on what's truly essential, and an opportunity to model for her children that life is meant to be lived fully. When you're willing to step outside conventional approaches to both business and family life, you create possibilities that others might never consider.

Your next step? Consider what adventures, values, or experiences you've been putting on hold until "someday." Kathleen's story is permission to start integrating those priorities now, not after you've achieved some arbitrary level of success or stability.

Entrepreneurship gave Kathleen her power back not because it made her wealthy or famous, but because it allowed her to align her daily actions with her deepest values. It gave her the flexibility to show up for her family, the autonomy to serve her community in meaningful ways, and the confidence that comes from creating something valuable with your own hands and mind.

That same power is available to you, whether through traditional entrepreneurship, intrapreneurship within existing organizations, or simply by applying entrepreneurial thinking to how you manage your current roles and responsibilities. The question isn't whether you have what it takes—it's whether you're ready to step into the power you already possess.
